title: Aulosepalum oestlundii

This epiphytic orchid is native to the tropical regions of Central America, specifically in Costa Rica and Panama. It belongs to the genus Aulosepalum, which is a small group of orchids characterized by their unique flowers and growth habits. The species Aulosepalum oestlundii is further classified into various subspecies, each with distinct characteristics. Within the Orchidaceae family, Aulosepalum oestlundii is part of the tribe Cranichideae, which includes several other orchid species.

Aulosepalum oestlundii is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
